extra.message: User input from sqli.get("username") is interpolated directly into SQL string using f-string in cursor.execute(). This allows SQL injection. An attacker can bypass authentication or extract data by providing malicious input like: admin' OR '1'='1

cwe: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ("SQL Injection")

extra.message: User input from ssti.get("mathexp") is concatenated directly into Jinja2 Template string. This allows Server-Side Template Injection (SSTI) which can lead to Remote Code Execution. An attacker can execute arbitrary code via payloads like: {{7*7}} or {{config.items()}}

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ("Code Injection")

extra.message: The fetchfile() function opens files using open(name) without any path sanitization. Combined with lfivuln() which takes user-controlled filename input, this allows Path Traversal to read arbitrary files. An attacker can read sensitive files using payloads like: ../../../etc/passwd

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ("Path Traversal")

extra.message: The fetchimage() function makes HTTP requests to user-controlled URLs via requests.get(url=name). Combined with rfivuln() taking imagelink from user input, this allows Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF). An attacker can make the server request internal resources, cloud metadata endpoints, or scan internal networks.

User input from xss.get("username") is reflected directly in the JSON response without sanitization. If this response is rendered in an HTML context without proper escaping, it enables Cross-Site Scripting (XSS). An attacker can inject malicious scripts via payloads like: <script>alert(1)</script>

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ("Cross-site Scripting")

extra.message: The Host header from request.headers is reflected directly into HTML output within an anchor href attribute. This allows HTTP Header Injection and XSS if an attacker can control the Host header. An attacker could inject: evil.com' onclick='alert(1)' x='

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ("Cross-site Scripting")
CWE-113: Improper Neutralization of CRLF Sequences in HTTP Headers

extra.message: Hardcoded database credentials found: mike/kaines and admin/admin are inserted into USERS table. Hardcoded credentials can be exposed in source code repositories and compromise system security.
Flask application runs with debug=True enabled. Debug mode exposes sensitive information, enables the interactive debugger, and can lead to Remote Code Execution via the Werkzeug debugger console.

cwe: CWE-215: Insertion of Sensitive Information Into Debugging Code

extra.message: SSL certificate verification is disabled with verify=False in requests.get(). This allows Man-in-the-Middle attacks as the server will accept any certificate, including self-signed or forged certificates.

cwe: CWE-599: Missing Validation of OpenSSL Certificate
